description_long: This fork features a long, straight handle with a rectangular cross-section, tapering slightly towards the tines. The surface is mottled with a dark, weathered patina, suggesting age or heavy use, and the finish appears matte with visible surface irregularities. The fork has four evenly spaced, straight tines that are parallel to each other, with a slight curve at the tips for practical use. The handle is unadorned, lacking any decorative engravings or branding, and its flat, broad design provides a sturdy grip. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a robust and somewhat rustic aesthetic, likely made from metal. The fork is shown alongside a matching knife and spoon, all sharing the same material and design language.
description: A weathered metal fork with a long, rectangular handle and four straight, parallel tines. The surface is dark, mottled, and matte, giving it a rustic, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the fork, with four straight tines and a flat, rectangular handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the fork and the slight curvature of the tines.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the fork, handle, and tines, mirroring the front but with less detail on the tines.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the fork's profile and the gentle curve of the tines from another angle.
attribute_handle_design: The handle is long, flat, and rectangular with a slight taper towards the tines, featuring a matte, mottled surface and no decorative elements.
attribute_tine_alignment: The tines are straight, parallel, and evenly spaced, with a slight curve at the tips.
